111	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
112	
113	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
114	
115	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
116	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
117	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
118	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
119	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
120	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
121	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
122	      map_add' := fun x y => by
123	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
124	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
125	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
126	        congr 1
127	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
128	          (C := C) f r }
129	  map_id U := by
130	    ext x
131	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
132	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
133	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
134	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
135	    ext x
136	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
137	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
138	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
139	
140	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
141	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
142	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
143	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
144	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
145	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
146	  rfl
147	
148	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
149	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
150	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
151	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
152	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
153	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
154	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
155	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1
156	
157	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
158	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
159	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
160	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
161	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
162	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
163	
164	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
165	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
166	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
167	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
168	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
169	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
170	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
171	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
172	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
173	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
174	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
175	  Iso.refl _
176	
177	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
178	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
179	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
180	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
181	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
182	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
183	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
184	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
185	noncomputable abbrev HModule
186	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
187	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
188	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
189	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
190	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
191	
192	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
193	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
194	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
195	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any
196	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
197	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
198	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
199	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
200	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
201	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a
202	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
203	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
204	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
205	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
206	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
207	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
208	    HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k]
209	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :=
210	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
211	
212	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
213	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
214	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
215	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
216	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
217	
218	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
219	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
220	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
221	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
222	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` — under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
223	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
224	
225	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
226	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
227	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
228	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
229	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
230	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
231	iter-015+. -/
232	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
233	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
234	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
235	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
236	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
237	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
238	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
239	
240	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
241	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
242	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
243	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
244	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`
245	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
246	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
247	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
248	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
249	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
250	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
251	derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
252	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
253	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
254	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
255	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
256	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
257	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
258	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
259	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
260	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
261	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) :=
262	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
263	
264	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
265	H⁰ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
266	(ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
267	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
268	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the
269	Hom group on the RHS, but we want the Hom hypothesis on the LHS and the
270	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
271	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
272	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
273	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
274	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/
275	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero
276	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
277	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
278	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
279	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
280	    [Module.Finite k ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)] :
281	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
282	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm
283	
284	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule' k F 0 X`. Parallel
285	of `Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised carrier
286	`HModule'`. The iter-015 H⁰ bridge `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv : HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
287	((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X) ⟶ F)` identifies the
288	degree-zero open-evaluation cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom group from the sheafified
289	representable; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness
290	across. The `.symm` is required for the same orientation reason as in
291	`Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero`. -/
292	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero
293	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
294	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Ben Eltschig. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Ben Eltschig
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.ConstantSheaf
9	
10	/-!
11	# Global sections of sheaves
12	
13	In this file we define a global sections functor `Sheaf.Γ : Sheaf J A ⥤ A` and show that it
14	is isomorphic to several other constructions when they exist, most notably evaluation of sheaves
15	on a terminal object and `Functor.sectionsFunctor`.
16	
17	## Main definitions / results
18	
19	* `HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A`: typeclass stating that the constant sheaf functor `A ⥤ Sheaf J A`
20	  has a right-adjoint.
21	* `Sheaf.Γ J A`: the global sections functor `Sheaf J A ⥤ A`, defined as the right-adjoint of the
22	  constant sheaf functor, whenever that exists.
23	* `constantSheafΓAdj J A`: the adjunction between the constant sheaf functor and `Sheaf.Γ J A`.
24	* `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections J A hT`: on sites with a terminal object `T`, `Sheaf.Γ J A` exists and
25	  is isomorphic to the functor evaluating sheaves at `T`.
26	* `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoLim J A`: when `A` has limits of shape `Cᵒᵖ`, `Sheaf.Γ J A` exists and is isomorphic
27	  to the functor taking each sheaf to the limit of its underlying presheaf.
28	* `Sheaf.isLimitConeΓ F`: global sections are limits even when not all limits of shape `Cᵒᵖ` exist.
29	* `Sheaf.ΓRes F U`: the restriction morphism from global sections of `F` to sections of `F` on `U`.
30	* `Sheaf.natTransΓRes J A U`: the natural transformation from the global sections functor to
31	  the sections functor on `U`.
32	* `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSectionsFunctor J`: for sheaves of types, `Sheaf.Γ J A` is isomorphic to the
33	  functor taking each sheaf to the type of sections of its underlying presheaf in the sense of
34	  `Functor.sections`.
35	* `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoCoyoneda J`: for sheaves of types, `Sheaf.Γ J A` is isomorphic to the
36	  coyoneda embedding of the terminal sheaf, i.e. the functor sending each sheaf `F` to the type
37	  of morphisms from the terminal sheaf to `F`.
38	
39	## TODO
40	
41	* Generalise `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSectionsFunctor` and `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoCoyoneda` from `Type max u v` to
42	  `Type max u v w`. This should hopefully be doable by relaxing the universe constraints of
43	  `instHasSheafifyOfHasFiniteLimits`.
44	
45	-/
46	
47	@[expose] public section
48	
49	universe u v w u₂ v₂
50	
51	open CategoryTheory Limits Sheaf Opposite GrothendieckTopology
52	
53	namespace CategoryTheory
54	
55	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
56	  (A : Type u₂) [Category.{v₂} A] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
57	
58	/-- Typeclass stating that the constant sheaf functor has a right adjoint. This right adjoint will
59	then be called the global sections functor and written `Sheaf.Γ`. -/
60	abbrev HasGlobalSectionsFunctor := (constantSheaf J A).IsLeftAdjoint
61	
62	/-- We define the global sections functor as the right-adjoint of the constant sheaf functor
63	whenever it exists. -/
64	noncomputable def Sheaf.Γ [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] : Sheaf J A ⥤ A :=
65	  (constantSheaf J A).rightAdjoint
66	deriving Functor.IsRightAdjoint
67	
68	/-- The constant sheaf functor is by definition left-adjoint to the global sections functor. -/
69	noncomputable def constantSheafΓAdj [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] :
70	    constantSheaf J A ⊣ Γ J A :=
71	  Adjunction.ofIsLeftAdjoint (constantSheaf J A)
72	
73	/-- Sites with a terminal object admit a global sections functor. -/
74	instance hasGlobalSectionsFunctor_of_hasTerminal [HasTerminal C] :
75	    HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A :=
76	  ⟨_, ⟨constantSheafAdj J A terminalIsTerminal⟩⟩
77	
78	/-- On sites with a terminal object, the global sections functor is isomorphic to the functor
79	of sections on that object. -/
80	noncomputable def Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections [HasTerminal C] {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
81	    Γ J A ≅ (sheafSections J A).obj (op T) :=
82	  (constantSheafΓAdj J A).rightAdjointUniq (constantSheafAdj J A hT)
83	
84	/-- Every site `C` admits a global sections functor for `A`-valued sheaves when `A` has limits of
85	shape `Cᵒᵖ`. -/
86	instance hasGlobalSectionsFunctor_of_hasLimitsOfShape [HasLimitsOfShape Cᵒᵖ A] :
87	    HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A :=
88	  ⟨sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ lim, ⟨constLimAdj.comp (sheafificationAdjunction J A)⟩⟩
89	
90	/-- Global sections of sheaves are naturally isomorphic to the limits of the underlying presheaves.
91	Note that while `HasLimitsOfShape Cᵒᵖ A` is needed here to talk about `lim` as a functor, global
92	sections are still limits without it - see `Sheaf.isLimitConeΓ`. -/
93	noncomputable def Sheaf.ΓNatIsoLim [HasLimitsOfShape Cᵒᵖ A] :
94	    Γ J A ≅ sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ lim :=
95	  (constantSheafΓAdj J A).rightAdjointUniq (constLimAdj.comp (sheafificationAdjunction J A))
96	
97	variable {J A}
98	
99	/-- Natural transformations from a constant presheaf into a sheaf correspond to morphisms to its
100	global sections. -/
101	noncomputable def Sheaf.ΓHomEquiv [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] {X : A} {F : Sheaf J A} :
102	    ((Functor.const _).obj X ⟶ F.obj) ≃ (X ⟶ (Γ J A).obj F) :=
103	  ((sheafificationAdjunction J A).homEquiv _ _).symm.trans
104	    ((constantSheafΓAdj J A).homEquiv _ _)
105	
106	/-- Naturality lemma for `ΓHomEquiv` analogous to `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left`. -/
107	lemma Sheaf.ΓHomEquiv_naturality_left [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] {X' X : A} {F : Sheaf J A}
108	    (f : X' ⟶ X) (g : (Functor.const _).obj X ⟶ F.obj) :
109	    ΓHomEquiv ((Functor.const _).map f ≫ g) = f ≫ ΓHomEquiv g :=
110	  (congrArg _ ((sheafificationAdjunction J A).homEquiv_naturality_left_symm _ _)).trans
111	    ((constantSheafΓAdj J A).homEquiv_naturality_left _ _)
112	
113	/-- Naturality lemma for `ΓHomEquiv` analogous to `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm`. -/
114	lemma Sheaf.ΓHomEquiv_naturality_left_symm [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] {X' X : A} {F : Sheaf J A}
115	    (f : X' ⟶ X) (g : X ⟶ (Γ J A).obj F) :
116	    ΓHomEquiv.symm (f ≫ g) = (Functor.const _).map f ≫ ΓHomEquiv.symm g :=
117	  (congrArg _ ((constantSheafΓAdj J A).homEquiv_naturality_left_symm _ _)).trans
118	    ((sheafificationAdjunction J A).homEquiv_naturality_left _ _)
119	
120	/-- Naturality lemma for `ΓHomEquiv` analogous to `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right`. -/
121	lemma Sheaf.ΓHomEquiv_naturality_right [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] {X : A} {F F' : Sheaf J A}
122	    (f : (Functor.const _).obj X ⟶ F.obj) (g : F ⟶ F') :
123	    ΓHomEquiv (f ≫ g.hom) = ΓHomEquiv f ≫ (Γ J A).map g :=
124	  (congrArg _ ((sheafificationAdjunction J A).homEquiv_naturality_right_symm _ _)).trans
125	    ((constantSheafΓAdj J A).homEquiv_naturality_right _ _)
126	
127	/-- Naturality lemma for `ΓHomEquiv` analogous to `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm`. -/
128	lemma Sheaf.ΓHomEquiv_naturality_right_symm [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] {X : A}
129	    {F F' : Sheaf J A} (f : X ⟶ (Γ J A).obj F) (g : F ⟶ F') :
130	    ΓHomEquiv.symm (f ≫ (Γ J A).map g) = ΓHomEquiv.symm f ≫ g.hom :=
131	  (congrArg _ ((constantSheafΓAdj J A).homEquiv_naturality_right_symm _ _)).trans
132	    ((sheafificationAdjunction J A).homEquiv_naturality_right _ _)
133	
134	/-- The cone over a given sheaf whose cone point is the global sections and whose components are
135	the restriction maps. -/
136	@[simps pt]
137	noncomputable def Sheaf.coneΓ [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] (F : Sheaf J A) : Cone F.obj where
138	  pt := (Γ J A).obj F
139	  π := ΓHomEquiv.symm (𝟙 _)
140	
141	/-- The global sections cone `Sheaf.coneΓ` is limiting - that is, global sections are limits even
142	when not all limits of shape `Cᵒᵖ` exist in `A`. -/
143	noncomputable def Sheaf.isLimitConeΓ [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] (F : Sheaf J A) :
144	    IsLimit F.coneΓ where
145	  lift c := F.ΓHomEquiv c.π
146	  fac c j := by
147	    suffices h : ((Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).map (ΓHomEquiv c.π)) ≫ F.coneΓ.π = c.π from congr_app h j
148	    simp [coneΓ, ← ΓHomEquiv_naturality_left_symm]
149	  uniq c f hf := by
150	    replace hf : ((Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).map f) ≫ F.coneΓ.π = c.π := by ext j; exact hf j
151	    simpa [coneΓ, ← ΓHomEquiv_naturality_left_symm, Equiv.symm_apply_eq] using hf
152	
153	/-- The restriction map from global sections of `F` to sections on `U`. -/
154	noncomputable def Sheaf.ΓRes [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] (F : Sheaf J A) (U : Cᵒᵖ) :
155	    (Γ J A).obj F ⟶ F.obj.obj U :=
156	  F.coneΓ.π.app U
157	
158	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
159	lemma Sheaf.ΓRes_map [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] (F : Sheaf J A) {V U : Cᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
160	    F.ΓRes U ≫ F.obj.map f = F.ΓRes V :=
161	  F.coneΓ.w f
162	
163	@[simp]
164	lemma Sheaf.coneΓ_π_app [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] (F : Sheaf J A) (U : Cᵒᵖ) :
165	    F.coneΓ.π.app U = F.ΓRes U := rfl
166	
167	lemma Sheaf.ΓRes_naturality [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] {F G : Sheaf J A} (f : F ⟶ G) (U : Cᵒᵖ) :
168	    (Γ J A).map f ≫ ΓRes G U = ΓRes F U ≫ f.hom.app U := by
169	  refine .trans ?_ <| congr_app (ΓHomEquiv_naturality_right_symm _ _) U
170	  exact (congr_app (ΓHomEquiv_naturality_left_symm ((Γ J A).map f) (𝟙 _)) U).symm.trans (by simp)
171	
172	variable (J A)
173	
174	/-- The natural transformation from the global sections functor to the sections functor on any
175	object `U`. -/
176	@[simps!]
177	noncomputable def Sheaf.natTransΓRes [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A] (U : Cᵒᵖ) :
178	    Γ J A ⟶ (sheafSections J A).obj U where
179	  app F := ΓRes F U
180	  naturality _ _ f := ΓRes_naturality f U
181	
182	/-- Global sections of a sheaf of types correspond to sections of the underlying presheaf. -/
183	noncomputable def Sheaf.ΓObjEquivSections [HasWeakSheafify J (Type w)]
184	    [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (Type w)] (F : Sheaf J (Type w)) :
185	      (Γ J (Type w)).obj F ≃ F.obj.sections :=
186	  (Equiv.trans (by exact (Equiv.funUnique (PUnit) _).symm.trans TypeCat.homEquiv.symm)
187	    ΓHomEquiv.symm).trans (F.obj.sectionsEquivHom PUnit).symm
188	
189	lemma Sheaf.ΓObjEquivSections_naturality [HasWeakSheafify J (Type w)]
190	    [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (Type w)] {F G : Sheaf J (Type w)} (f : F ⟶ G)
191	    (x : (Γ J (Type w)).obj F) :
192	    (ΓObjEquivSections J G) ((Γ J _).map f x) =
193	      (Functor.sectionsFunctor _).map f.hom ((ΓObjEquivSections J F) x) := by
194	  dsimp [ΓObjEquivSections]
195	  exact (congr_arg _ (ΓHomEquiv_naturality_right_symm (TypeCat.ofHom (uniqueElim x)) f)).trans
196	    (Functor.sectionsEquivHom_naturality_symm _ _ _)
197	
198	lemma Sheaf.ΓObjEquivSections_naturality_symm [HasWeakSheafify J (Type w)]
199	    [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (Type w)] {F G : Sheaf J (Type w)} (f : F ⟶ G)
200	    (x : F.obj.sections) : (ΓObjEquivSections J G).symm ((Functor.sectionsFunctor _).map f.hom x) =
201	      (Γ J _).map f ((ΓObjEquivSections J F).symm x) :=
202	  ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (ΓHomEquiv_naturality_right (F.obj.sectionsEquivHom _ x) f) _
203	
204	/-- For sheaves of types, the global sections functor is isomorphic to the sections functor
205	on presheaves. -/
206	noncomputable def Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSectionsFunctor :
207	    Γ J (Type (max u v)) ≅ sheafToPresheaf J _ ⋙ Functor.sectionsFunctor _ :=
208	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun F ↦ (ΓObjEquivSections J F).toIso) fun f ↦ by
209	    ext x
210	    exact ΓObjEquivSections_naturality J f x
211	
212	/-- Global sections of a sheaf of types `F` correspond to morphisms from a terminal sheaf to `F`.
213	We use the constant sheaf on a singleton type as a specific choice of terminal sheaf here. -/
214	noncomputable def Sheaf.ΓObjEquivHom [HasWeakSheafify J (Type w)]
215	    [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (Type w)] (F : Sheaf J (Type w)) (X : Type w)

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

section
variable {k : Type} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))

-- Sheaf.Γ.obj F ≅ F.obj.obj (op ⊤) categorically
noncomputable example :
    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{0} k)).obj
      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
    ≅ ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
  (Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
    (ModuleCat.{0} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)

-- The categorical iso gives a LinearEquiv on underlying
-- Module.Finite is preserved across categorical isos in ModuleCat
example (M N : ModuleCat.{0} k) (e : M ≅ N) (h : Module.Finite k M) :
    Module.Finite k N :=
  Module.Finite.equiv (e.toLinearEquiv) (M := M) (N := N)

-- Hom from base ring identification
example (M : ModuleCat.{0} k) :
    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
  ModuleCat.homEquiv.trans (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M.carrier)
end

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

section
universe u
variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))

-- Categorical iso (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F ≅ F.obj.obj (op ⊤)
noncomputable def gammaIso (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
      ≅ F.val.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : Opens C.left.toTopCat)) :=
  (Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
    (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F

-- For F = toModuleKSheaf C, F.val.obj (op ⊤) = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))
noncomputable example :
    (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).val.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : Opens C.left.toTopCat))
    = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := rfl

-- LinearEquiv from categorical iso in ModuleCat k
noncomputable example (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F).carrier
    ≃ₗ[k] F.val.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : Opens C.left.toTopCat)) :=
  (gammaIso C F).toLinearEquiv

end

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between Sheaf.Γ-applied carrier and the value at the
top open. Underlying iso is `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` (categorical, in 
`ModuleCat k`); converted to `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv`. -/
noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
      ≃ₗ[k] F.val.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : Opens X)) :=
  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv

/-- Iter-045 immediate consumer combining iter-044 + the gamma-eval LinearEquiv. -/
theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
    Module.Finite k
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
  haveI := module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
  exact Module.Finite.equiv
    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

    = C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) := rfl

    Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)))
    → Module.Finite k
      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).val.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) := id

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

-- Forward map: ((constantSheaf @ k) ⟶ F) → F.val.obj (op ⊤)
section
variable (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
variable (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k))

noncomputable example (g : (constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
    (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) : 
    ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F : ModuleCat k) :=
  ((constantSheafΓAdj _ _).homEquiv _ _ g).hom (1 : k)

-- The forward map respects k-scalars: g ↦ unit ≫ Sheaf.Γ.map g, evaluated at 1
-- We want: forward(r • g) = r • forward(g)
-- By definition of • on LHS (Sheaf.linear), (r • g).val = r • g.val.
-- Sheaf.Γ.map (r • g) = ?  (this is where Functor.Linear k (Sheaf.Γ) is needed)

-- Try directly: 
example (r : k) (g : (constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
    (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :
    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).map (r • g) =
      r • (Sheaf.Γ _ _).map g := by sorry

end
end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory Limits

universe u

variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Type u} [Category.{u, u} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]

-- Try: provide Functor.Additive (presheafToSheaf J _) directly
noncomputable instance presheafToSheaf_additive :
    Functor.Additive (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) := by
  -- Left adjoint of additive functor is additive (sheafToPresheaf is additive trivially)
  exact (sheafificationAdjunction J _).leftAdjointAdditive

-- Then constantSheaf is additive (composition of additive functors)
noncomputable instance constantSheaf_additive :
    Functor.Additive (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) := by
  unfold constantSheaf
  exact Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _

-- Then Sheaf.Γ is additive (right adjoint of constantSheaf, which is additive)
noncomputable instance sheafGamma_additive [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] :
    Functor.Additive (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) := by
  exact (constantSheafΓAdj J _).rightAdjointAdditive

#check @Adjunction.leftAdjointAdditive

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Sophie Morel. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Sophie Morel, Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Yoneda.Basic
9	
10	/-!
11	# Adjunctions between additive functors.
12	
13	This provides some results and constructions for adjunctions between functors on
14	preadditive categories:
15	* If one of the adjoint functors is additive, so is the other.
16	* If one of the adjoint functors is additive, the equivalence `Adjunction.homEquiv` lifts to
17	  an additive equivalence `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`.
18	* We also give a version of this additive equivalence as an isomorphism of `preadditiveYoneda`
19	  functors (analogous to `Adjunction.compYonedaIso`), in `Adjunction.compPreadditiveYonedaIso`.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	universe u₁ u₂ v₁ v₂
26	
27	namespace CategoryTheory
28	
29	namespace Adjunction
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Category Functor
32	
33	variable {C : Type u₁} {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₁} C] [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive C]
34	  [Preadditive D] {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj : F ⊣ G)
35	
36	include adj
37	
38	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
39	lemma right_adjoint_additive [F.Additive] : G.Additive where
40	  map_add {X Y} f g := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by simp [homEquiv_counit])
41	
42	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
43	lemma left_adjoint_additive [G.Additive] : F.Additive where
44	  map_add {X Y} f g := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by simp [homEquiv_unit])
45	
46	variable [F.Additive]
47	
48	/-- If we have an adjunction `adj : F ⊣ G` of functors between preadditive categories,
49	and if `F` is additive, then the hom set equivalence upgrades to an `AddEquiv`.
50	Note that `F` is additive if and only if `G` is, by `Adjunction.right_adjoint_additive` and
51	`Adjunction.left_adjoint_additive`.
52	-/
53	def homAddEquiv (X : C) (Y : D) : AddEquiv (F.obj X ⟶ Y) (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
54	  { adj.homEquiv _ _ with
55	    map_add' _ _ := by
56	      have := adj.right_adjoint_additive
57	      simp [homEquiv_apply] }
58	
59	@[simp]
60	lemma homAddEquiv_apply (X : C) (Y : D) (f : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
61	    adj.homAddEquiv X Y f = adj.homEquiv X Y f := rfl
62	
63	@[simp]
64	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_apply (X : C) (Y : D) (f : X ⟶ G.obj Y) :
65	    (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm f = (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f := rfl
66	
67	@[simp]
68	lemma homAddEquiv_zero (X : C) (Y : D) : adj.homEquiv X Y 0 = 0 := map_zero (adj.homAddEquiv X Y)
69	
70	@[simp]
71	lemma homAddEquiv_add (X : C) (Y : D) (f f' : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
72	    adj.homEquiv X Y (f + f') = adj.homEquiv X Y f + adj.homEquiv X Y f' :=
73	  map_add (adj.homAddEquiv X Y) _ _
74	
75	@[simp]
76	lemma homAddEquiv_sub (X : C) (Y : D) (f f' : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
77	    adj.homEquiv X Y (f - f') = adj.homEquiv X Y f - adj.homEquiv X Y f' :=
78	  map_sub (adj.homAddEquiv X Y) _ _
79	
80	@[simp]
81	lemma homAddEquiv_neg (X : C) (Y : D) (f : F.obj X ⟶ Y) :
82	    adj.homEquiv X Y (-f) = - adj.homEquiv X Y f := map_neg (adj.homAddEquiv X Y) _
83	
84	@[simp]
85	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_zero (X : C) (Y : D) :
86	    (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm 0 = 0 := map_zero (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm
87	
88	@[simp]
89	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_add (X : C) (Y : D) (f f' : X ⟶ G.obj Y) :
90	    (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm (f + f') = (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f + (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f' :=
91	  map_add (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm _ _
92	
93	@[simp]
94	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_sub (X : C) (Y : D) (f f' : X ⟶ G.obj Y) :
95	    (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm (f - f') = (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f - (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f' :=
96	  map_sub (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm _ _
97	
98	@[simp]
99	lemma homAddEquiv_symm_neg (X : C) (Y : D) (f : X ⟶ G.obj Y) :
100	    (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm (-f) = - (adj.homEquiv X Y).symm f :=
101	  map_neg (adj.homAddEquiv X Y).symm _
102	
103	open Opposite in
104	/-- If we have an adjunction `adj : F ⊣ G` of functors between preadditive categories,
105	and if `F` is additive, then the hom set equivalence upgrades to an isomorphism between
106	`G ⋙ preadditiveYoneda` and `preadditiveYoneda ⋙ F`, once we throw in the necessary
107	universe lifting functors.
108	Note that `F` is additive if and only if `G` is, by `Adjunction.right_adjoint_additive` and
109	`Adjunction.left_adjoint_additive`.
110	-/
111	def compPreadditiveYonedaIso :
112	    G ⋙ preadditiveYoneda ⋙ (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{max v₁ v₂} ≅
113	      preadditiveYoneda ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op ⋙
114	        (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{max v₁ v₂} :=
115	  NatIso.ofComponents
116	    (fun Y ↦ NatIso.ofComponents
117	      (fun X ↦ (AddEquiv.ulift.trans ((adj.homAddEquiv (unop X) Y).symm.trans
118	        AddEquiv.ulift.symm)).toAddCommGrpIso)
119	      (fun g ↦ by
120	        ext ⟨y⟩
121	        exact AddEquiv.ulift.injective (adj.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm g.unop y)))
122	    (fun f ↦ by
123	      ext _ ⟨x⟩
124	      exact AddEquiv.ulift.injective ((adj.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm x f)))
125	
126	lemma compPreadditiveYonedaIso_hom_app_app_apply (X : Cᵒᵖ) (Y : D)
127	    (a : ULift.{max v₁ v₂, v₁} (Opposite.unop X ⟶ G.obj Y)) :
128	      ((adj.compPreadditiveYonedaIso.hom.app Y).app X) a =
129	        ULift.up ((adj.homEquiv (Opposite.unop X) Y).symm (AddEquiv.ulift a)) := rfl
130	
131	lemma compPreadditiveYonedaIso_inv_app_app_apply (X : Cᵒᵖ) (Y : D)
132	    (a : ULift.{max v₁ v₂, v₂} (F.obj (Opposite.unop X) ⟶ Y)) :
133	      ((adj.compPreadditiveYonedaIso.inv.app Y).app X) a =
134	        ULift.up ((adj.homEquiv (Opposite.unop X) Y) (AddEquiv.ulift a)) := rfl
135	
136	end Adjunction
137	
138	end CategoryTheory

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

-- Probe: forward map from Hom group to F.val.obj (op ⊤)
section
variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
variable (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k))

-- The unit of sheafification at the constant presheaf
noncomputable def myUnit : (Functor.const _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶
    ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
      ((Functor.const _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k))).val :=
  (sheafificationAdjunction (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).unit.app
    ((Functor.const _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k))

-- forward: g ↦ ((unit ≫ g.val).app (op ⊤)).hom 1
noncomputable example
    (g : (constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj 
      (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :
    F.val.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
  ((myUnit F).app (Opposite.op ⊤) ≫ g.val.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom 1

-- Probe: is this map k-linear in g?
-- By definition (r • g).val = r • g.val (Sheaf.linear is component-wise on underlying)
-- so (myUnit ≫ (r • g.val)).app (op ⊤) = myUnit.app (op ⊤) ≫ r • g.val.app (op ⊤)
--                                       = r • (myUnit.app ≫ g.val.app)
-- So yes it's linear in g.
end

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

section
variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
variable (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k))

-- Try evaluating the Hom morphism at op ⊤ and applying to an element
noncomputable example
    (g : (constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj 
      (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :
    F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
  -- The underlying nat trans is g.hom; its value at op ⊤ is a morphism in ModuleCat k
  -- We need to determine: from what to F.obj.obj (op ⊤)?
  -- (constantSheaf @ k).obj.obj (op ⊤) ⟶ F.obj.obj (op ⊤)
  let h := g.hom.app (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X))
  -- h : (constantSheaf @ k).obj.obj (op ⊤) ⟶ F.obj.obj (op ⊤) in ModuleCat k
  -- Now apply to: the unit-image of 1 from the sheafification
  let unit_app := (sheafificationAdjunction (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).unit.app
        ((Functor.const _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k))
  let u := unit_app.app (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X))
  -- u : (Functor.const _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k).obj (op ⊤) ⟶ (sheafification.obj _).obj.obj (op ⊤)
  -- The first is just `ModuleCat.of k k` (constant value), the second is `(constantSheaf @ k).obj.obj (op ⊤)`
  (h.hom (u.hom (1 : k)))

end

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

-- Probe: use F.obj instead of F.val (post-deprecation API)
section
variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
variable (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k))

-- F.obj is the new spelling for F.val
example : F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) = 
    F.val.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) := rfl

-- Use F.obj
noncomputable def myLinearEquivTop :
    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
      ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv

end

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between the global-sections module
`(Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F` (an object of `ModuleCat k`) and the underlying carrier
of `F.obj.obj (op ⊤)` for any sheaf `F` on a topological space `X`. The
underlying iso comes from `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` (Mathlib): on a site
with terminal `T`, the global-sections functor is naturally iso to evaluation
at `T`. For `X.toTopCat`, the terminal in `Opens X` is `⊤`. The categorical iso
in `ModuleCat k` is converted to a `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv`. -/
noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
      ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv

adjunction, which is multi-iteration project-local infrastructure. -/
theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
    Module.Finite k
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
  haveI : Module.Finite k
      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
    module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
  exact Module.Finite.equiv
    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm

```
noncomputable def Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections [HasTerminal C] {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
    Γ J A ≅ (sheafSections J A).obj (op T) :=
  (constantSheafΓAdj J A).rightAdjointUniq (constantSheafAdj J A hT)

#### Declaration 1: Global-sections evaluation iso — `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`

**Probe-confirmed body** (this pass; copy verbatim — note the short name does NOT carry the `Scheme.` prefix because the enclosing `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` already opens that segment):

```lean
/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between the global-sections module
`(Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F` (an object of `ModuleCat k`) and the underlying carrier
of `F.obj.obj (op ⊤)` for any sheaf `F` on a topological space `X`.

The underlying iso comes from `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` (Mathlib
`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/GlobalSections.lean`): on a site with terminal
`T`, the global-sections functor is naturally iso to evaluation at `T`. For
the topology of opens `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, the terminal in
`TopologicalSpace.Opens X` is the top open `⊤` (this is `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
for any preorder with a top element). The categorical iso in `ModuleCat k` is
converted to a `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's standard
upgrading of `ModuleCat`-isos to LinearEquivs).

Iter-046+ uses this `LinearEquiv` together with the linearised constant-sheaf
/ global-sections adjunction (multi-iteration; project-local lift of
Mathlib's `Adjunction.homAddEquiv` to `≃ₗ[k]`) to construct the producer
instance `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
      ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv
```

#### Declaration 2: `Module.Finite` consumer — `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper`

**Probe-confirmed body** (this pass; copy verbatim):

```lean
/-- Iter-045 immediate consumer: combining iter-044's geometric Stein input
`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper` with `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`
yields `Module.Finite k ((Sheaf.Γ).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` for a proper
integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`.

The `haveI` is necessary because `Module.Finite.equiv` does not
auto-synthesise the `[Module.Finite k]` hypothesis on the source: the iter-044
declaration's conclusion is `Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))`,
but the source of `(SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top _ _).symm` is
`(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)` — these are *the same Module* (via the
iter-006 `toModuleKPresheaf_obj` simp lemma) but Lean needs the `haveI` to
register the typeclass under the new spelling.

This is the algebraic input for the iter-046+ producer-instance assembly:
bridging from `Sheaf.Γ.obj` to the Hom group `((constantSheaf @ unit) ⟶ -)`
requires the linearised constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction, which is multi-iteration
project-local infrastructure. -/
theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
    Module.Finite k
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
  haveI : Module.Finite k
      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
    module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
  exact Module.Finite.equiv
    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm
```
